description:
When I meet Junior, I’m not just looking at a dangerous dog.
I’m looking at a dangerous contradiction.
Inside the home, he’s calm, loving, and deeply connected to the family, especially to a young boy he helps through panic attacks. But outside, it’s a completely different story. Junior has bitten multiple people and even lunged at a toddler. And that tells me something important.
This isn’t about aggression.
It’s about responsibility.
Junior has taken on a role that doesn’t belong to him. When
title: How to Stop a Dog From Biting People at the Dog Park | Cesar 911
